title: AI-901 Mock Exam 2: Workloads, Agents, and Implementation
description: Unofficial AI-901 revision set focused on AI workloads, responsible AI, Microsoft Foundry, agents, text, speech, vision, and information extraction.

items.prompt: A customer service system must identify product names and order numbers in support emails. Which text analysis technique is most relevant?
items.answer: Entity detection

items.prompt: A news app needs one paragraph from a long article. Which technique should it use?
items.answer: Summarization

items.prompt: A retail app needs to detect whether reviews are positive or negative. Which technique fits?
items.answer: Sentiment analysis

items.prompt: A compliance team wants to find important themes in complaints without reading every sentence. Which technique helps?
items.answer: Key phrase extraction

items.prompt: Which speech capability lets a meeting app create transcripts from audio?
items.answer: Speech to text

items.prompt: Which speech capability lets an app read generated answers aloud?
items.answer: Text to speech

items.prompt: A travel kiosk must accept spoken user requests and respond with generated audio. Which combination is most relevant?
items.answer: Speech recognition, model response, and speech synthesis
options: Speech recognition, model response, and speech synthesis

items.prompt: A warehouse app must count boxes in photos. Which workload is most relevant?
items.answer: Computer vision

items.prompt: A marketing app creates banner concepts from natural language prompts. Which model capability is used?
items.answer: Image generation

items.prompt: A field app sends a photo and asks what safety hazard is visible. Which capability is most relevant?
items.answer: Visual input interpretation by a multimodal model
options: Visual input interpretation by a multimodal model

items.prompt: A mortgage app extracts names, totals, and dates from scanned forms. Which Foundry Tools area fits best?
items.answer: Content Understanding for documents and forms
options: Content Understanding for documents and forms

items.prompt: Which scenario uses information extraction from audio or video?
items.answer: Finding speakers, topics, and key moments in a recorded call
options: Finding speakers, topics, and key moments in a recorded call

items.prompt: Which responsible AI principle is involved when testing an app under expected and unexpected conditions before release?
items.answer: Reliability and safety

items.prompt: A loan approval AI must avoid systematically disadvantaging protected groups. Which principle is central?
items.answer: Fairness

items.prompt: An AI app explains its data sources and limitations to users. Which principle does this support?
items.answer: Transparency

items.prompt: A product owner assigns people to review risky AI decisions and handle appeals. Which principle is supported?
items.answer: Accountability

items.prompt: What is the best reason to use a system prompt?
items.answer: Set durable behavior, role, constraints, and safety rules for the model
options: Set durable behavior, role, constraints, and safety rules for the model

items.prompt: Which user prompt is most effective?
items.answer: Summarize this support ticket in three bullets and include the customer sentiment
options: Summarize this support ticket in three bullets and include the customer sentiment

items.prompt: A model keeps returning customer summaries in inconsistent formats. The app requires one paragraph followed by three action items. What should the developer add to the prompt?
items.answer: Examples that demonstrate the required response pattern and format
options: Examples that demonstrate the required response pattern and format

items.prompt: A model must return valid JSON for an app to parse. What should the prompt include?
items.answer: The required output schema and constraints
options: The required output schema and constraints

items.prompt: A developer needs a lightweight chat client using Foundry. What should the app generally call?
items.answer: The Foundry project endpoint through SDK, REST, or an OpenAI-compatible client
options: The Foundry project endpoint through SDK, REST, or an OpenAI-compatible client

items.prompt: A developer is writing a lightweight Python app that calls a Foundry project endpoint from Azure-hosted compute. Which authentication pattern is most appropriate?
items.answer: Use Azure identity credentials with the Foundry project client
options: Use Azure identity credentials with the Foundry project client
Embed an administrator password in the prompt
Use anonymous access for the endpoint
Store the private key in browser local storage

items.prompt: Which authentication method is preferable for cloud-hosted production apps in Azure?
items.answer: Managed identity or Microsoft Entra-based credentials where supported
options: Managed identity or Microsoft Entra-based credentials where supported
Public environment variables in frontend code

items.prompt: What should a developer test after deploying a model in Foundry?
items.answer: That prompts and application calls reach the deployment and return expected outputs
options: That prompts and application calls reach the deployment and return expected outputs
Only that a JSON file exists

items.prompt: Which model parameter should be lowered when a deterministic, repeatable style is more important than creative variation?
items.answer: Temperature

items.prompt: Which model parameter helps prevent extremely long responses?
items.answer: Maximum output tokens

items.prompt: Which model choice is best for complex multi-step reasoning if cost and latency are less important?
items.answer: A more capable reasoning model
options: A smaller model chosen only for speed

items.prompt: Which model choice is often best for high-volume simple classification tasks?
items.answer: A smaller, faster, lower-cost model that meets quality needs
options: A smaller, faster, lower-cost model that meets quality needs

items.prompt: Which Foundry feature helps browse and choose available models from multiple providers?
items.answer: Model catalog
options: Key vault secret version list only

items.prompt: What is a tool call in an agent workflow?
items.answer: A model-selected request to an external capability, function, or data source
options: A model-selected request to an external capability, function, or data source

items.prompt: An agent needs to answer using a company's policy PDFs. Which tool is most suitable?
items.answer: File search or retrieval over those documents
options: File search or retrieval over those documents

items.prompt: An agent must calculate totals from a CSV. Which tool is most suitable?
items.answer: Code interpreter

items.prompt: An agent needs live external facts from the web, where allowed. Which tool category is relevant?
items.answer: Web search

items.prompt: What is the main difference between a prompt agent and hosted agent?
items.answer: Prompt agents are configuration-based and managed; hosted agents run custom agent code
options: Prompt agents are configuration-based and managed; hosted agents run custom agent code

items.prompt: Why use agent versioning or publishing?
items.answer: To provide stable, controlled agent releases for applications or users
options: To provide stable, controlled agent releases for applications or users

items.prompt: Which observability signal helps debug why an agent chose a tool?
items.answer: Trace of the agent steps and tool calls
options: Trace of the agent steps and tool calls

items.prompt: What does RBAC help control in Foundry and Azure?
items.answer: Which identities can access or manage resources and operations
options: Which identities can access or manage resources and operations

items.prompt: Why should API keys or secrets not be placed in frontend React code?
items.answer: They can be exposed to users and attackers through the browser bundle
options: They can be exposed to users and attackers through the browser bundle

items.prompt: Which AI workload is most likely used to classify an image as damaged or undamaged?
items.answer: Computer vision image classification

items.prompt: Which AI workload is most likely used to locate each product in a shelf image?
items.answer: Object detection

items.prompt: Which workload is most likely used to answer questions about the contents of a video?
items.answer: Multimodal or content understanding over video
options: Multimodal or content understanding over video

items.prompt: A user asks an AI app for legal advice. Which design response best supports responsible AI?
items.answer: Provide boundaries, encourage professional advice, and avoid unsupported claims
options: Provide boundaries, encourage professional advice, and avoid unsupported claims

items.prompt: Which testing approach best supports fairness?
items.answer: Evaluate performance across relevant user groups and data slices
options: Evaluate performance across relevant user groups and data slices

items.prompt: Which practice helps protect private data in logs?
items.answer: Avoid logging sensitive prompts and redact personal data where needed
options: Avoid logging sensitive prompts and redact personal data where needed

items.prompt: A model returns outdated product pricing. What would most likely help?
items.answer: Ground responses with a current trusted data source
options: Ground responses with a current trusted data source

items.prompt: A developer creates a small web app with a text box and a submit button. The app sends the user's question to a Foundry agent endpoint and displays the response. What has the developer built?
items.answer: A lightweight client application for an AI agent
options: A lightweight client application for an AI agent

items.prompt: Which task is closest to implementing text analysis with Foundry?
items.answer: Build an app that sends text to an appropriate AI service or model and displays extracted insights
options: Build an app that sends text to an appropriate AI service or model and displays extracted insights

items.prompt: Which task is closest to implementing vision capabilities with Foundry?
items.answer: Build an app that sends image input to a capable model or vision tool and handles the result
options: Build an app that sends image input to a capable model or vision tool and handles the result

items.prompt: Which task is closest to implementing information extraction with Content Understanding?
items.answer: Configure extraction over documents or media and use the structured output in an app
options: Configure extraction over documents or media and use the structured output in an app

items.prompt: A team previously built simple Azure AI demos. They now need to deploy models, create agents, call them from code, and use Foundry Tools for speech, vision, and extraction. Which platform should they use for the implementation work?
items.answer: Microsoft Foundry
